beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, scan MLS listings on Mercer Island.
beats.reasoning: Linda's ritual start: early rise, coffee, MLS obsession. Consistent with her recent pattern of pre-dawn energy and deal-hunting mindset.

beats.activity: Power walk around Mercer Island, calls to clients.
beats.reasoning: Warm Friday perfect for her signature power walk; she multitasks by calling clients. Consistent with her recent days and networking drive.

beats.activity: Commute to Bellevue office via I-90 and I-405.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning drive from Mercer Island to Bellevue; accounts for 45 min cross-lake traffic on I-90 to I-405 ramp, arriving before team standup.

beats.activity: Team meeting and emails at Windermere Bellevue office.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning briefing, client emails, and open house prep. Linda is always plugged into office rhythm before heading out to showings.

beats.activity: Commute to downtown Seattle open house.
beats.reasoning: Drive from Bellevue to downtown Seattle for morning open house; ~35–40 min via I-405 to I-90 West. Pride street closures won't impact her yet (they start at 5pm).

beats.activity: Host open house in downtown Seattle, network with agents and buyers.
beats.reasoning: Linda hosts open houses regularly; Friday morning in downtown draws foot traffic. Warm day brings out buyers; she networks and logs interest.

beats.activity: Lunch with developer contact at upscale restaurant.
beats.reasoning: Linda's pattern: high-value lunches over deals. Ascend is her go-to for developer networking. Fuel for the afternoon and relationship maintenance.
Drive from downtown Seattle to Medina (~25–30 min via I-90 East and local roads). This is her 'highlight' area with serious money; Friday afternoon is prime showing time.

beats.activity: Host luxury waterfront open house in Medina, meet high-net-worth buyers.
beats.reasoning: Warm Friday afternoon attracts serious buyers to waterfront properties. Linda thrives here; recent days show Medina is her highest-energy territory. Warm weather and breezy conditions showcase outdoor spaces beautifully.

beats.activity: Drive back to Bellevue office to catch up on emails and close out the day.
beats.reasoning: Return to Bellevue base (~30 min from Medina via I-405). Linda consolidates afternoon notes, logs showings, and prepares for weekend market activity.

beats.activity: Office work: client calls, CRM updates, weekend open house prep.
beats.reasoning: Linda is a power agent; afternoon office time for follow-ups and admin. She prepares weekend open house signage and confirms Saturday/Sunday showings.

beats.activity: Commute home to Mercer Island, avoid downtown Pride street closures.
beats.reasoning: Friday evening drive home; Linda avoids downtown entirely to skip Pride street closures (Broadway/Pike closed from 5pm). She takes I-405 south to I-90 East to Mercer Island, arriving home by 6:15pm.

beats.activity: Evening at home: hydrate, light dinner, review MLS for weekend showings, wind down.
beats.reasoning: Linda spends warm Friday evening on Mercer Island overlooking water; she hydrates after a full day, eats light, scrolls MLS for Saturday/Sunday opportunities, and relaxes before bed. Consistent with her recent evening pattern.
7.5 hours rest before a busy Saturday of open houses and showings. Sleep ends early in the morning to resume her pre-dawn ritual.

diary:
What a day. Started before dawn with coffee and MLS scrolling—Mercer Island's market is hot right now, and I needed to stay ahead. The waterfront path was perfect this morning, crisp and quiet before the rush. Clients called while I walked; that's how you stay connected.

The open house near Pike Place was buzzing with energy, and lunch with the developer contact paid off—whispers of a new mixed-use project coming to Capitol Hill. Speaking of which, I deliberately routed home through Medina to avoid the Pride street closures downtown. Nothing against the celebration, but Broadway and Pike were already packed by mid-afternoon. The luxury waterfront showing went well; those buyers looked serious.

By evening I was home, feet up, reviewing listings for the weekend. The city felt alive out there today—warm, breezy, full of possibility. It's the kind of Friday that reminds me why I love this work.
thought_process: I prioritized back-to-back showings today because the market's moving fast and my reputation depends on being present. The early walk-and-call routine keeps me sharp and connected. I consciously chose the Medina route home around 3pm because I knew Pride would clog the downtown core—not out of any judgment, just logistics. The developer lunch felt strategically important; I'm always hunting for intelligence on upcoming projects that could mean new listings. By evening, I was genuinely tired but satisfied. The warm weather and festive energy in the city lifted my mood even as I stayed focused on work. I ended the day planning rather than partying, which feels right for where I am in my career and life.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
